summary: All 3 NDIS Yarns background rows returned licence-safe person-free candidates (13 offered: civic 5, brand-texture 4, data-grid 4); PARTIAL reflects NON-PP out-of-proven-scope brand (candidate-level scrutiny) + a genuinely thin/marginal data-grid row (CC0 alone was not harvestable — Pexels-licence used).

candidates.creator: Mineia Martins
candidates.attribution_text: Photo by Mineia Martins on Pexels
candidates.visual_description: Abstract blue glass office facade filling the frame: a reflective curtain-wall window grid in deep navy/blue tones, geometric and sky-free.
candidates.text_safe_notes: Self-check 1080 centre-crop @0.55 scrim: even mid-detail window grid, no single focal object competing at centre; deep navy reads well behind white/teal headline. Legible.
warnings: uniform window-grid detail spans the whole frame; confirm headline contrast under scrim

candidates.creator: Jan van der Wolf
candidates.attribution_text: Photo by Jan van der Wolf on Pexels
candidates.visual_description: Teal glass curtain wall with regular vertical brass/gold mullions on a diagonal composition.
candidates.text_safe_notes: Self-check: regular vertical striping, low focal clutter; teal matches brand palette; gold mullions add a warm accent. Legible.
warnings: regular diagonal lines may interfere with a centred text baseline

candidates.creator: Jan van der Wolf
candidates.attribution_text: Photo by Jan van der Wolf on Pexels
candidates.visual_description: Modern glass building corner against a clear blue sky; upper area is clean open sky.
candidates.text_safe_notes: Self-check: upper sky provides clean negative space, centre is the building edge; scrim keeps headline legible. Best placed mid-lower.
warnings: bright high-key sky at top; keep headline off the brightest sky zone

candidates.creator: Mikitayo
candidates.visual_description: Sweeping curved silver/white modern architecture — a smooth metal-and-glass curve (portrait framing).
candidates.text_safe_notes: Self-check: smooth curved surfaces, low text competition, cool neutral tone; legible under scrim.
warnings: some bright highlights along the curve

candidates.creator: Ronald van Eendenburg
candidates.attribution_text: Photo by Ronald van Eendenburg on Pexels
candidates.visual_description: Teal-green rectangular cladding/glass panels in regular diagonal rows (building-facade texture).
candidates.text_safe_notes: Self-check: regular low-detail panel grid, teal-green brand-compatible, even under scrim. Legible.
warnings: regular panel seams; verify no moire against text

candidates.creator: Allec Gomes
candidates.attribution_text: Photo by Allec Gomes on Pexels
candidates.visual_description: Deep navy silky soft waves — a smooth dark gradient texture with very low detail across the whole frame.
candidates.text_safe_notes: Self-check: dark uniform navy negative space everywhere; ideal scrim backdrop; excellent headline legibility. Matches brand navy.

candidates.creator: Eva Bronzini
candidates.attribution_text: Photo by Eva Bronzini on Pexels
candidates.visual_description: Dark teal-to-navy horizontal gradient wash with a subtle teal band.
candidates.text_safe_notes: Self-check: very low detail, dark, clean; brand teal/navy; excellent overlay legibility.

candidates.creator: Eva Bronzini
candidates.attribution_text: Photo by Eva Bronzini on Pexels
candidates.visual_description: Deep navy near-solid gradient (a dark blue night-sky wash).
candidates.text_safe_notes: Self-check: almost flat dark navy, maximal negative space; excellent headline legibility.

candidates.creator: UP Modern
candidates.attribution_text: Photo by UP Modern on Pexels
candidates.visual_description: Predominantly black frame with a soft lavender/blue light curve in the lower-left (3D render).
candidates.text_safe_notes: Self-check: predominantly black negative space, minimal detail; headline sits clean over black.
warnings: accent curve leans lavender/purple — slightly off the navy/teal palette; predominantly black so low impact

candidates.creator: Evija Ciematniece
candidates.attribution_text: Photo by Evija Ciematniece on Pexels
candidates.visual_description: Radial blue light streaks emanating from a dark central vanishing point on deep navy — a calm motion/information-flow register.
candidates.text_safe_notes: Self-check: the dark centre is clean negative space with the headline zone in the calmest area; streaks radiate to the edges. Legible if headline kept central.
warnings: radial streaks toward the edges; keep headline within the central dark zone

candidates.creator: Solen Feyissa
candidates.attribution_text: Photo by Solen Feyissa on Pexels
candidates.visual_description: Teal connected lines and scattered dots/bubbles on black — a connected-dots / node register.
candidates.text_safe_notes: Self-check: large black negative space; the dots-and-lines motif reads as network/connection; legible.
warnings: some brighter dot clusters in the upper area

candidates.creator: Valentin Ivantsov
candidates.attribution_text: Photo by Valentin Ivantsov on Pexels
candidates.visual_description: Bright blue folded line-mesh grid surface — an abstract grid/mesh.
candidates.text_safe_notes: Self-check: the grid/mesh fills the frame and is brighter/busier; competes more with a headline; calmest near edges. Alternate only.
warnings: bright saturated blue and busy mesh lower text-contrast; offered as an alternate, not for the calmest layouts

candidates.creator: Merlin
candidates.visual_description: Two glowing wireframe network spheres joined by a luminous bridge on teal (plexus/node render).
candidates.text_safe_notes: Self-check: the bright connection glows through the centre and competes with a centred headline; corners are darker. Consider a lower/off-centre headline.
warnings: bright centre bridge reduces central legibility

rejected_reasons: cc0_pool_off_category_irrelevant (4: rust-infected leaves / arcade machines / blue clipart) — NOT harvestable from CC0 alone

non_claims: nothing approved/uploaded/inserted; image-reviewer then PK visual review is the only deciding act
licence-hold (CC BY / CC BY-SA), paid-premium and AI-generated material recorded-conceptually and never offered — none appear in this package
no DB/storage/git/pool/deploy surface touched; writes confined to _harness/image_harvester_v0/ndis_bg_20260717/**
creator names were read from the Pexels source listing pages, not re-confirmed on each individual photo page — flagged for reviewer

should_fix: data-grid row is thin and text-safety-marginal: -03 (bright mesh) is alternate-only lower-contrast; -04 has a bright central node bridge — reviewer/PK should weigh whether the data-grid register clears the bar or should be re-scoped.
re-confirm each creator on its individual Pexels photo page (names taken from listing pages).
observations: No people/faces, no flags/crests/gov-identifying buildings, no readable third-party signage, and no legible text survived the 1080 centre-crop @0.55 scrim in any offered candidate.
No First-Nations / cultural visual language and no medical/clinical disability stereotypes offered — scope was deliberately person-free/abstract.
Brand-texture and civic rows are strong; brand-texture best-picks (deep navy/teal washes) give the cleanest overlay negative space.
CC0-only sourcing under-served all three rows (civic pool skewed to interior-design renders; data-grid CC0 was off-target); on-target candidates came from Pexels-licence stock, which policy permits.
findings_contract.scope_boundary: NON-Property-Pulse brand (NDIS Yarns) — outside the image-harvester PROVEN scope, so first-attempt candidate-level scrutiny applies. Person-free / abstract scoping was chosen deliberately to stay clear of the people-forward disability category and the First-Nations sensitive category (no cultural sign-off exists for this lane). Sources searched: Openverse CC0/PDM API and Pexels-licence CDN; Unsplash and Wikimedia Commons were not deeply mined this pass. Nothing here is approved.
open_questions: Is Pexels-licence stock acceptable for NDIS Yarns, or does this non-PP brand require CC0-only? (CC0 alone was insufficient, especially for data-grid.)
Does the data-grid 'information/connection' register suit a compliance-strict explainer brand, or should it be dropped in favour of civic + brand-texture only?
findings_contract.recommended_next_gate: image-reviewer pass, then PK visual review (NON-PP → candidate-level scrutiny)
non_claims: nothing approved/uploaded/inserted; image-reviewer then PK visual review is the only deciding act
licence-hold (CC BY / CC BY-SA), paid-premium and AI-generated material recorded-conceptually and never offered — none appear in this package
no DB/storage/git/pool/deploy surface touched; writes confined to _harness/image_harvester_v0/ndis_bg_20260717/**
